requirements.summary: Create a frontend service module that queries the RepeaterBook public API and maps responses to the app's internal Repeater shape
acceptanceCriteria: Module exports a function (e.g., fetchRepeaterBookData) accepting state abbreviation, optional city, and optional frequency parameters
Function constructs correct query URL to https://www.repeaterbook.com/api/export.php with appropriate parameters
Response JSON is parsed and mapped to the app's internal Repeater shape
Returned objects include a source field set to 'repeaterbook'
Module handles network errors gracefully and returns an empty array on failure

file_operations.description: Create a service module that calls the RepeaterBook public API at https://www.repeaterbook.com/api/export.php. Export a fetchRepeaterBookData function that accepts state (2-letter abbreviation), optional city, and optional frequency parameters. Construct the query URL with state_id, city, and frequency parameters as needed. Parse the JSON response and map RepeaterBook fields (Frequency, Offset, Uplink Tone, Downlink Tone, TSQ, Call Sign, Nearest City, State, Use, Operational Status, etc.) to the app's Repeater type, adding a source field set to 'repeaterbook'. Handle network errors gracefully by catching exceptions and returning an empty array on failure. Use appropriate TypeScript types for function parameters and return values.

requirements.summary: Integrate RepeaterBook API into the Directory Page to fetch and display live repeater data alongside local repeaters
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting a state in the filter bar triggers a RepeaterBook API fetch for that state
Entering a city triggers a RepeaterBook API fetch for that city
A loading spinner or indicator is shown while the RepeaterBook API request is in-flight
RepeaterBook results render in the same RepeaterCard grid as local repeaters
Each card clearly displays a 'RepeaterBook' or 'Local' source badge
Local (user-submitted) repeaters continue to display alongside RepeaterBook results

file_operations.description: Import the fetchRepeaterBookData function from frontend/src/services/repeaterBookApi.ts. Add state to track RepeaterBook loading status and fetched results. When the user selects a state or enters a city in the filter bar, call fetchRepeaterBookData with the appropriate parameters and set loading state. Display a loading spinner while the API request is in-flight. Merge RepeaterBook results with local repeaters from getApprovedRepeaters query, ensuring both sources coexist in the same display. Pass the source field to RepeaterCard so it can display appropriate badges. Maintain existing local repeater display logic without removing or breaking it.

Add support for displaying a source badge on each repeater card. Check for a 'source' field on the repeater prop (set to either 'repeaterbook' or undefined for local). Render a visible badge or label indicating 'RepeaterBook' for live data or 'Local' for user-submitted repeaters. Style the badges consistently with the existing dark ham-radio theme using amber/orange accents for RepeaterBook and a contrasting color for Local. Position the badge prominently on the card (e.g., top-right corner or near the call sign).

requirements.summary: Update RepeaterDetailModal to handle RepeaterBook-sourced repeaters, display N/A for missing fields, and disable favoriting for external data
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the detail modal for a RepeaterBook entry renders all available fields correctly
Missing fields on RepeaterBook entries display 'N/A' instead of blank or undefined values
The favorite button is visually disabled for RepeaterBook entries
A tooltip on the disabled favorite button explains that favoriting is only available for locally submitted repeaters
The favorite button remains fully functional for local repeater entries

file_operations.description: Check if the repeater has a source field set to 'repeaterbook'. For RepeaterBook entries, display 'N/A' for any missing or undefined fields instead of showing blank values. Disable the favorite button when source is 'repeaterbook' by passing a disabled prop to FavoriteButton or wrapping it conditionally. Add a tooltip (using a title attribute or a UI library tooltip component) explaining that favoriting is only available for locally submitted repeaters. Ensure the favorite button remains fully functional for local repeater entries (those without a source field or with source set to something other than 'repeaterbook').

Add support for a disabled prop that visually disables the button and prevents click actions. When disabled, apply appropriate styling (e.g., reduced opacity, gray color) and prevent the add/remove favorite mutations from being called. Ensure the tooltip prop or title attribute is displayed when the button is disabled to inform users why favoriting is unavailable.

requirements.summary: Add an informational banner on the Directory Page crediting RepeaterBook.com and explaining manual GMRS submission
acceptanceCriteria: An informational banner is displayed on the Directory Page above or below the filter bar
Banner text states that live ham radio data is sourced from RepeaterBook.com
Banner text states that GMRS repeaters can be submitted via the submission form
A link to https://www.repeaterbook.com is present and opens in a new tab
Banner styling matches the existing dark navy/charcoal ham-radio theme with amber/orange accents

file_operations.description: Add a prominent informational banner component near the top of the Directory Page, positioned above or below the FilterBar. The banner should contain text stating that live ham radio repeater data is sourced from RepeaterBook.com and that GMRS repeaters can be submitted manually via the submission form. Include a clickable link to https://www.repeaterbook.com that opens in a new tab (using target='_blank' and rel='noopener noreferrer'). Style the banner using Tailwind classes consistent with the existing dark navy/charcoal ham-radio theme, applying amber or orange accent colors (e.g., bg-navy-900, text-amber-400, border-amber-500) to match the app's visual identity. Use padding, borders, and rounded corners to make the banner visually distinct but cohesive with the page design.
